synovial_fluid_volume_mL.comment: Normal human knee synovial fluid volume 0.5-4 mL; inflamed gout knee can be 10-50 mL with effusion. Use 2-4 mL as the 'after IA injection mixing' default. Range covers small joints (MTP first joint typical gout flare site: 0.1-1 mL) to large knee effusions.

joint_capsule_surface_area_cm2.comment: Approximate inner-capsule surface area for human knee; smaller joints (MTP1, MCP) scale down ~10×.

cartilage_thickness_mm.comment: Human knee articular cartilage thickness range; relevant for the diffusion distance from synovial fluid to chondrocytes.

synovial_fluid_volume_mL.comment: First MTP joint — the canonical gout flare site (podagra). Smaller volume than knee; higher concentration of injected enzyme dose.

urate_substrate_concentration_at_msu_sur.comment: Plasma urate in hyperuricemia: 0.4-0.7 mM. Synovial fluid urate: similar 0.4-0.7 mM. AT the MSU crystal surface, urate is at thermodynamic saturation/supersaturation; the local mass-transfer-limited concentration available to a uricase at the crystal-solution interface is approximately at saturation (~0.4-0.6 mM at 37 °C, pH 7.4) since the crystal acts as an infinite reservoir while it persists. Sustained uricase action can deplete the bulk synovial fluid urate down to sub-saturation, but local-to-crystal concentration remains saturated. For comp-035, use the local-saturation concentration as the URATE input driving H2O2 production at the architecture site. Range 0.5-5 mM covers solution depletion (low end) to high tophi-density crystal cluster (high end, where multiple crystals supply substrate).

joint_clearance_h2o2_rate_per_s.comment: First-order rate constant for joint-fluid H2O2 clearance from endogenous sources (lymphatic drainage, endogenous catalase / glutathione peroxidase / peroxiredoxins in synoviocytes, vascular uptake). Estimated; not directly measured for H2O2. The intrinsic synovial-clearance half-life of small solutes is hours-to-days, but H2O2 has additional enzymatic destruction pathways. Range covers slow joint clearance (0.001/s = 700 s half-life) to fast endogenous-enzymatic clearance (0.05/s = 14 s half-life). This is treated as a background sink in the diffusion model — IA-injected catalase architectures must reduce [H2O2] BELOW the threshold ON TOP OF the endogenous clearance, not on its own.

joint_temperature_C.comment: Inflamed gouty joint may be slightly warmer (38-39 °C); use 37 °C as the baseline.
